intro: The clearest split between Plaid and Prove is how they charge: Plaid uses subscription pricing, while Prove uses custom enterprise pricing (no public tier). Plaid is Financial data network connecting apps to 12,000+ banks for account verification, income data, and identity checks; it fits best for Fintech, Marketplace. Prove is Prove is a digital identity platform using phone-number intelligence, possession checks and Prove Pre-Fill to verify and authenticate users, cutting fraud and friction in onboarding for banks and fintechs; it fits best for US banks and fintechs using phone possession to reduce fraud and autofill KYC fields. Pick Plaid when the job maps to fintech requiring identity verification; pick Prove when it maps to phone-number intelligence for frictionless kyc onboarding. Plaid is not for consumer apps without regulatory requirements. Prove is not for global or privacy-minimal products that cannot rely on us carrier data for verification. Honest trade-offs: Plaid is weak for consumer apps without regulatory requirements, and Prove is weak for us-centric, weaker outside north america — match to your real scenario.
quick_take: Plaid is for fintech, marketplace; Prove is for us banks and fintechs using phone possession to reduce fraud; decide based on pricing model.
verdict_a: Choose Plaid if you want subscription pricing and your workload matches fintech, marketplace.
verdict_b: Choose Prove if you want custom enterprise pricing (no public tier) and your workload matches us banks and fintechs using phone possession to reduce fraud and autofill kyc fields.

faq.a: Plaid has more transparent pricing; Prove's cost depends on a sales conversation, so a direct comparison is not possible without a quote. Plaid uses subscription pricing with no free tier published. Prove uses custom enterprise pricing with no published rates.

faq.q: Can I migrate from Plaid to Prove?
faq.a: Migration may be non-trivial: Prove is rated high lock-in (Custom identity contracts). Plan for data export, re-integration of SDKs, and re-testing flows before cutover. Verify exportable formats and feature parity against your live use cases before committing.

Plaid scores higher on developer experience (5/5 vs 4/5 for Prove). Evaluate against your own stack: read their docs, run a quick SDK spike, and check error handling. Self-assessing with a small prototype beats trusting any rating at face value.

faq.q: Is Prove a good alternative to Plaid?
faq.a: Prove sits in the same category as Plaid, so it can be a reasonable alternative for overlapping use cases. Prove is best fit for us banks and fintechs using phone possession to reduce fraud and autofill kyc fields. Note: Prove is not for global or privacy-minimal products that cannot rely on us carrier data for verification.
